mappings.label: SpectraSync Algorithm
mappings.formula: Multi-channel intensity calculation based on growth stage, DLI target, and spectral recipe. Channels: CW/WW (cool/warm white), 450nm (blue), 660nm (red), 730nm (far-red). Formula: intensity[channel] = min(100, (targetDLI / currentDLI) * recipe[channel] * stageFactor)
mappings.validation: Each channel 0-100, total PPFD <= fixture capacity

mappings.label: DLI (Daily Light Integral)
mappings.formula: DLI = (PPFD × photoperiod_hours × 3600) / 1,000,000. PPFD from fixture spec × intensity%. Result in mol/m²/day
mappings.validation: min: 10, max: 65 mol/m²/day (typical cannabis range: 20-50)

mappings.label: Photoperiod
mappings.formula: Format: "hours_on/hours_off" (e.g., "18/6" for veg, "12/12" for flower). Parsed to light_duration and dark_duration
mappings.validation: Pattern: /^\d{1,2}\/\d{1,2}$/, total must equal 24

mappings.label: Sunrise/Sunset Ramp
mappings.formula: Linear intensity ramp from 0% to target over N minutes at day start, reverse at day end. Step = (target_intensity / ramp_minutes)
mappings.validation: min: 0, max: 120 minutes

mappings.label: Channel Intensity (Grow3)
mappings.formula: HEX12 encoding: intensity_percent → hex = Math.round((intensity / 100) * 64).toString(16).padStart(2, "0"). Range: 00-40 (0-64 steps)
mappings.validation: 0-100% input, encoded as 00-40 hex (0-64 decimal)

mappings.label: PPFD Calculation
mappings.formula: Actual PPFD = ppfd_max × (intensity / 100) × fixture_count × coverage_factor. Coverage_factor ≈ 0.8-1.0 depending on canopy uniformity
mappings.validation: Fixture PPFD max typically 800-2000 μmol/m²/s

mappings.label: Light Count per Room
mappings.formula: User input × fixture area → total coverage. Validation: total_coverage >= room_area
mappings.validation: min: 1, max: 500 per room

mappings.label: Channel Calibration Factor
mappings.formula: Measured PPFD / Expected PPFD = factor. Applied as: actual_intensity = requested_intensity × factor
mappings.validation: min: 0.5, max: 1.5 (50-150% of nominal)

mappings.label: Spectra to 4-Channel Conversion
mappings.source: Plan spectra definition → 4-channel tunable light (CW, WW, 450nm, 660nm)
mappings.formula: Convert spectral recipe to 4 channels: CW (cool white 5000-6500K) = base_white × (1 - warm_factor), WW (warm white 2700-3500K) = base_white × warm_factor, Blue_450nm = blue_percentage / 100, Red_660nm = red_percentage / 100. Where base_white = 1 - (blue_percentage + red_percentage) / 100, warm_factor from plan.spectrum.warmth (0=cool, 1=warm). Example: "Veg" spectrum (30% blue, 10% red, 60% white with warmth=0.3) → CW=42%, WW=18%, Blue=30%, Red=10%
mappings.validation: Sum of all channels should normalize to 100% total; each channel 0-100%

mappings.label: PPFD Reduction to Match Plan DLI
mappings.source: Plan DLI target → fixture PPFD intensity adjustment
mappings.formula: Required_PPFD = (target_DLI × 1,000,000) / (photoperiod_hours × 3600). Intensity_percent = (Required_PPFD / fixture_max_PPFD) × 100. If calculated intensity > 100%, warn "DLI target unreachable with current fixture". Apply to all channels proportionally: channel_intensity = base_channel_percentage × (Intensity_percent / 100). Example: Plan DLI=35 mol/m²/day, photoperiod=18hrs, fixture_max=1000 μmol/m²/s → Required_PPFD=540 μmol/m²/s → Intensity=54%. If channel mix is CW=40%, WW=20%, Blue=30%, Red=10%, then actual output: CW=21.6%, WW=10.8%, Blue=16.2%, Red=5.4%
mappings.validation: DLI range: 10-65 mol/m²/day; photoperiod: 12-24 hours; fixture_max_PPFD: 500-2000 μmol/m²/s

mappings.label: Multi-Fixture PPFD Aggregation
mappings.source: Room with multiple fixtures → combined PPFD calculation
mappings.formula: Total_PPFD = Σ(fixture_ppfd_max[i] × intensity[i] / 100 × overlap_factor[i]). Overlap_factor accounts for fixture spacing: center coverage = 1.0, edge overlap = 0.7-0.9 depending on distance. For uniform DLI: keep all fixtures at same intensity unless doing zone-specific control
mappings.validation: Max practical total: 2000 μmol/m²/s (higher risks light stress); min useful: 200 μmol/m²/s

mappings.label: Fixture Spectrum Profile
mappings.source: device-kb.json → fixtures[].spectrum OR lights-seed.js → spectrum definition
mappings.formula: Spectrum object format: {channels: [{name: "CW", wavelength: "5000K", ppfd_contribution: 0.35}, {name: "450nm", wavelength: 450, ppfd_contribution: 0.30}, ...], total_ppfd_max: 1200, tunable: true/false}. For fixed-spectrum lights, channel percentages are constant. For tunable lights, percentages can be adjusted per channel
mappings.validation: Σ(ppfd_contribution) should equal 1.0; wavelength in nm or Kelvin; tunable flag must match hardware capability

mappings.label: Stage-Specific Spectrum Mixing
mappings.formula: Each growth stage defines spectrum mix: {blue_450nm: X%, red_660nm: Y%, white_ratio: Z%, far_red_730nm: W%}. Conversion to 4-channel: see spectra.to_4channel formula (id:33). Common recipes: Clone/Seedling (blue=40%, red=10%, white=50%), Veg (blue=30%, red=15%, white=55%), Early Flower (blue=20%, red=30%, white=50%), Late Flower (blue=10%, red=40%, white=50%)
mappings.validation: Total percentages should sum to 100%; each channel 0-100%; must align with fixture capabilities

mappings.label: PPFD Intensity Auto-Adjustment
mappings.source: Real-time DLI calculation vs plan target → intensity compensation
mappings.formula: Measure actual DLI: current_DLI = Σ(ppfd_samples × sample_interval) / 1,000,000 over photoperiod. Compare to target_DLI. If current_DLI  target_DLI × 1.1: decrease intensity by 1/compensation_factor (min 0.8x). Apply gradually over 15-min intervals to prevent shock
mappings.validation: Max single adjustment: ±20% intensity; max rate: 5% per 5 minutes; requires PPFD sensor feedback

mappings.label: AI Recommendation Engine
mappings.formula: ML model inputs: current_vpd, temp, humidity, light_hours, growth_stage, strain_profile. Outputs: suggested_adjustments (temp±2°F, humidity±5%, light±10%), confidence_score (0-1)
mappings.validation: Confidence threshold: 0.7 for auto-apply, 0.5 for suggestions

mappings.label: VPD-Based HVAC Control
mappings.formula: VPD = (SVP - AVP) / 1000, where SVP = 610.7 × 10^(7.5×T/(237.3+T)), AVP = SVP × (RH/100). Target VPD: veg=0.8-1.0 kPa, flower=1.0-1.4 kPa. Action: if VPD  target + 0.2 → decrease temp or increase humidity
mappings.validation: VPD range: 0.4-2.0 kPa; temp limits: 65-85°F; RH limits: 40-70%

mappings.label: Lux Balancing
mappings.formula: Target lux from env var (default: 800). For each zone: deviation = measured_lux - target_lux. If |deviation| > threshold (50 lux): adjust_intensity = current_intensity × (target_lux / measured_lux). Clamp to 0-100%
mappings.validation: Lux sensor range: 0-100,000; adjustment step max: ±10% per cycle

mappings.label: Occupancy Detection
mappings.formula: Motion sensor OR door sensor OR manual override. If occupied: brightness = OCCUPIED_BRIGHTNESS (100%); if vacant > 5min: brightness = VACANT_BRIGHTNESS (30%)
mappings.validation: Vacancy timeout: 5-60 minutes configurable

mappings.label: Growth Stage Detection
mappings.source: groups.json → [groupId].planConfig.anchor.stage OR calculated from seedDate + stage_durations
mappings.formula: If seedDate provided: days_since_seed = (now - seedDate) / 86400000. Stage progression: clone(0-14d) → veg(14-42d) → transition(42-56d) → flower(56-120d) → flush(120-127d). Else: user-selected stage
mappings.validation: Enum: clone, veg, transition, flower, flush

mappings.label: Spectral Recipe Engine
mappings.formula: Recipe per stage defines channel ratios. Example (flower): {CW: 60, WW: 40, 450nm: 30, 660nm: 80, 730nm: 10}. Normalized to max=100. Applied as: channel_output = (recipe[channel] / 100) × master_intensity
mappings.validation: Each channel 0-100; at least one channel > 0

mappings.label: Schedule Execution Loop
mappings.formula: Runs every 60s. For each active schedule: calculate current_time in photoperiod; determine light_state (on/off/ramp); fetch devices in group; apply intensity via /api/devicedatas PATCH. Log execution state
mappings.validation: Tick interval: 30-300 seconds

mappings.label: Harvest Date Prediction
mappings.formula: ML model inputs: strain_profile.flower_days, current_growth_rate (measured weekly), environmental_history (avg_temp, avg_vpd). Output: estimated_harvest = seedDate + veg_days + flower_days ± variance. Confidence interval: ±3-7 days
mappings.validation: Date range: seedDate + 60d to seedDate + 180d

mappings.label: Environmental Anomaly Detection
mappings.formula: Isolation Forest algorithm on 24h rolling window of temp, humidity, CO2, VPD. Features: mean, stddev, rate_of_change. Anomaly score > 0.6 triggers alert. Returns: [{timestamp, metric, score, severity}]
mappings.validation: Anomaly score: 0-1; severity: low/medium/high

mappings.label: Energy Efficiency Score
mappings.formula: kWh per gram dry yield. Inputs: total_electricity (sum of plug electricityOfDay), estimated_yield (grams). Score = total_kWh / estimated_yield. Benchmark: 1.5 = needs improvement
mappings.validation: min: 0.1, max: 10.0 kWh/g

mappings.label: Temperature (Celsius to Fahrenheit)
mappings.formula: (celsius × 9/5) + 32 = fahrenheit. Display with 1 decimal place
mappings.validation: Sensor range: -20 to 60°C (-4 to 140°F)

mappings.label: Humidity
mappings.formula: Direct passthrough from sensor (%). Smoothed with 5-sample moving average to reduce noise
mappings.validation: min: 0, max: 100%

mappings.label: VPD Calculation
mappings.formula: SVP = 610.7 × 10^((7.5 × temp_c)/(237.3 + temp_c)). AVP = SVP × (humidity/100). VPD = (SVP - AVP) / 1000 kPa
mappings.validation: Typical range: 0.4-2.0 kPa

mappings.label: Device Online Status
mappings.formula: Boolean flag. Updated on API poll (every 30-60s). True if lastSeen < 5 minutes ago
mappings.validation: True/false

mappings.label: Equipment Control Link
mappings.formula: Format: "IoT:" for linked plugs/switches, or static string (e.g., "Manual", "Wifi"). Normalized case-insensitively on save
mappings.validation: Must match iot-devices.json deviceId if prefixed with "IoT:"

mappings.label: Device Position (x, y)
mappings.source: room-map.json → devices[].x, y AND synced to iot-devices.json
mappings.formula: Grid coordinates. Origin (0,0) = top-left. x: 0 to gridSize-1, y: 0 to (gridSize×0.75)-1. Saved on drag-drop placement
mappings.validation: x: 0-gridSize, y: 0-(gridSize×0.75)

mappings.label: Interpolated Temperature Grid
mappings.source: Calculated from positioned sensors in room-map.json + telemetry
mappings.formula: Inverse Distance Weighting (IDW): temp(x,y) = Σ(temp_i / distance_i^2) / Σ(1 / distance_i^2). Power=2, max_distance=10 cells. Generates temp value for each grid cell
mappings.validation: Grid resolution: same as room map (default 20×15)

mappings.label: Plan Anchor (Seed Date)
mappings.formula: ISO 8601 date string. Used to calculate days-post-seed (DPS) = (now - seedDate) / 86400000. DPS drives stage auto-progression
mappings.validation: Valid ISO date; must be <= today; max age 365 days

mappings.label: Hold Duration (Days Post Seed)
mappings.formula: Number of days to maintain current settings before auto-advancing to next stage. If hold=14 and dps=14, stage transitions to next
mappings.validation: min: 0, max: 120 days

mappings.label: Schedule Active Days
mappings.formula: Array of day-of-week indices (0=Sunday, 6=Saturday). Schedule only executes on these days. Empty array = all days
mappings.validation: Each element 0-6, no duplicates

mappings.label: SwitchBot API Authentication
mappings.source: Environment vars: SWITCHBOT_TOKEN, SWITCHBOT_SECRET → HMAC-SHA256 signature
mappings.formula: t = timestamp (ms), nonce = UUID, sign = HMAC-SHA256(token + t + nonce, secret). Headers: {Authorization: token, t, sign, nonce}
mappings.validation: Token length: 64 chars; secret: 32 chars

mappings.label: Controller Target URL
mappings.source: Environment var: CTRL OR localStorage apiBase OR window.API_BASE
mappings.formula: Proxy base for /api/* and /controller/* routes. Example: http://192.168.2.80:3000. Falls back to location.origin
mappings.validation: Must be valid URL with http/https scheme

mappings.label: Anomaly Indoor Temperature
mappings.source: scripts/simple-anomaly-detector.py → env.json zones[] (filtered by room-map.json placement)
mappings.formula: SCOPE: Only sensors placed in Room Mapper (room-map.json devices[]). Reads env.json → zones[].sensors.tempC.history[] for zones where meta.deviceId matches room-map.json devices[].deviceId. Skips zones with "outdoor" in name. Filters out 0°C or null readings (sensor errors)
mappings.validation: Must be from physically placed sensor; excludes unplaced sensors in env.json

mappings.label: Anomaly Outdoor Temperature
mappings.formula: Outdoor sensor device ID hardcoded as CE2A83060A2C (East Outdoor Sensor). Reads current temperature from env.json → zones[] where meta.deviceId = CE2A83060A2C. Used as reference for all anomaly detections
mappings.validation: Outdoor sensor must be online and reporting to env.json

mappings.label: Outdoor-Aware Anomaly Detection
mappings.formula: Isolation Forest (sklearn) on feature matrix: [indoor_temp, indoor_rh, indoor_vpd, outdoor_temp, outdoor_rh, temp_delta]. Train on 24h history, contamination=0.03 (3% expected anomalies). Outdoor influence: assess_outdoor_influence() returns anomaly_likelihood (0-1). Severity: critical if likelihood>0.7, warning if >0.4, info otherwise. Critical examples: Indoor 28°C when outdoor 15°C (expected 18-22°C) = equipment failure
mappings.validation: Requires: outdoor sensor online, ≥50 historical samples, scikit-learn installed. Contamination: 0.01-0.1

mappings.label: Temperature Delta Analysis
mappings.formula: temp_delta = indoor_temp - outdoor_temp. Normal range: 3-10°C (HVAC maintaining indoor warmer than outdoor). If temp_delta  10°C → excessive heating or poor insulation. Used as feature in anomaly detection
mappings.validation: Typical delta: 3-10°C; extreme: 15°C indicates issues

mappings.label: Expected Indoor Range Calculation
mappings.formula: Based on outdoor temp, season, HVAC presence: Base range = outdoor_temp + offset. Summer offset: 5-8°C (cooling active). Winter offset: 8-12°C (heating active). Spring/Fall: 6-10°C. Returns (min_expected, max_expected) tuple. Example: outdoor 15°C → expected indoor 21-25°C. Deviations trigger anomaly investigation
mappings.validation: Season detected from month: Dec-Feb=winter, Mar-May=spring, Jun-Aug=summer, Sep-Nov=fall

mappings.label: HVAC Load Prediction
mappings.formula: Predicts HVAC system load (0-100%) based on indoor/outdoor delta and target setpoint. Cooling load: max(0, min(100, (indoor_temp - 22) × 10)) when outdoor > indoor. Heating load: max(0, min(100, (22 - indoor_temp) × 10)) when outdoor < indoor. Dehumidification: max(0, (indoor_rh - 60) × 2). Humidification: max(0, (50 - indoor_rh) × 2). Ventilation: based on temp_delta magnitude. Returns {cooling, heating, dehumidification, humidification, ventilation}
mappings.validation: Each load: 0-100%; used to contextualize anomalies (high load = normal if outdoor extreme)

mappings.label: Time-Lagged Outdoor Data
mappings.formula: Retrieves outdoor conditions from 15-60 minutes ago to account for thermal lag (building thermal mass delays indoor response to outdoor changes). Searches env.json history for outdoor sensor readings at timestamps: now - 15min, now - 30min, now - 60min. Returns {temp_lag15, temp_lag30, temp_lag60, rh_lag15, rh_lag30, rh_lag60}. Used as exogenous variables in forecasting models
mappings.validation: Requires sensor history in env.json; falls back to current outdoor if history unavailable

mappings.label: Solar Gain Factor
mappings.formula: Returns 0.0-1.0 based on time of day and season. Hour calculation: hour + minute/60. Night (0-6, 18-24): 0.0. Peak solar (9-15): 1.0. Sunrise ramp (6-9): (hour - 6) / 3. Sunset ramp (15-18): 1.0 - ((hour - 15) / 3). Season multiplier: summer×1.0, winter×0.6, spring/fall×0.8. Used to adjust expected indoor temp for solar heating through windows
mappings.validation: Returns float 0.0-1.0; peaks at noon in summer

mappings.label: Outdoor Influence Assessment
mappings.formula: Comprehensive outdoor-aware anomaly assessment. Inputs: indoor_temp, outdoor_temp, indoor_rh, outdoor_rh, zone_name, timestamp. Calculates: temp_delta, expected_indoor_range, hvac_load, solar_gain, time_lagged_outdoor. Determines outdoor_influence level: low (10°C). Computes anomaly_likelihood (0-1): score based on deviation from expected_range adjusted for solar_gain and hvac_load. Returns {temp_delta, outdoor_influence, anomaly_likelihood, expected_indoor_from_outdoor, hvac_load, reason}
mappings.validation: anomaly_likelihood: 0-1; outdoor_influence: low/medium/high; reason: descriptive string

mappings.label: Outdoor Sensor Data Quality
mappings.formula: Validates outdoor sensor data before ML processing. Temperature range: -40°C to 50°C (rejects 0°C sensor errors). Humidity range: 0-100%. Staleness: timestamp within 15 minutes. Returns {valid: boolean, temp: float, rh: float, source: "sensor"|"weather_api", validation_warnings: []}. If invalid, automatically falls back to weather API
mappings.validation: Staleness threshold: 15 min (configurable); temp sanity: ±5°C vs weather API; RH sanity: ±15% vs API

mappings.label: Weather API Fallback
mappings.formula: Fetches outdoor conditions from weather API when sensor fails. Endpoint: http://localhost:8091/api/weather?lat={farm_lat}&lng={farm_lng}. Extracts current temperature and relative humidity. Returns {temp: float, rh: float, source: "weather_api"}. Prevents corrupted sensor data from degrading ML model accuracy
mappings.validation: Requires farm coordinates in farm.json; API must return current.temperature_2m and current.relative_humidity_2m

mappings.label: SARIMAX Indoor Temperature Forecast
mappings.formula: SARIMAX (Seasonal AutoRegressive Integrated Moving Average with eXogenous variables) time-series model. Predicts indoor temp 1-4 hours ahead (5-min resolution). ARIMA order: (2,0,1) - AR(2), no differencing, MA(1). Seasonal order: (1,0,0,12) - Seasonal AR(1), 12 periods=1 hour. Exogenous features (8): outdoor_temp, outdoor_rh, outdoor_temp_lag15, outdoor_temp_lag30, outdoor_temp_lag60, solar_gain, hour_sin, hour_cos, is_weekend. Training: 72h history (288 samples). Returns predictions[] with {timestamp, value, lower_ci, upper_ci} at 95% confidence
mappings.validation: Requires ≥50 historical samples; hours_ahead: 1-4; typical accuracy: ±0.5°C (5-30min), ±1.0°C (30-60min), ±1.5°C (1-4hr)

mappings.label: SARIMAX Humidity Forecast
mappings.formula: Same SARIMAX architecture as temperature forecast (id:48) but for relative humidity. Predicts indoor RH 1-4 hours ahead. Uses same 8 exogenous variables. Training on 72h RH history. Accounts for outdoor humidity influence with time lags (15/30/60 min). Returns predictions[] with timestamp, value, confidence intervals
mappings.validation: RH range: 0-100%; typical accuracy: ±3% (5-30min), ±5% (30-60min), ±8% (1-4hr)

mappings.label: Forecast Exogenous Variables
mappings.formula: Feature engineering for SARIMAX model. Builds 8-column matrix: (1) outdoor_temp: current outdoor temperature, (2) outdoor_rh: current outdoor humidity, (3-5) outdoor_temp_lag15/30/60: time-lagged outdoor temps, (6) solar_gain: calculate_solar_gain_factor() 0.0-1.0, (7-8) hour_sin/cos: cyclical time encoding sin(2π×hour/24) and cos(2π×hour/24) to handle midnight wrap-around, (9) is_weekend: boolean 1 if Sat/Sun. All features resampled to 5-min intervals and forward/backward filled for missing values
mappings.validation: Feature matrix shape: (n_samples, 8); no NaN values; outdoor_temp must be available or fallback to weather API

mappings.label: Future Exogenous Generation
mappings.formula: Generates future exogenous features for forecast horizon. If weather_forecast provided (from API): maps hourly forecasts to 5-min intervals. Else: persists last known outdoor conditions. Time lags computed recursively: lag15[i] = outdoor_temp[i-3], lag30[i] = outdoor_temp[i-6], lag60[i] = outdoor_temp[i-12]. Solar gain recalculated for future timestamps. Hour encoding updated for future times. Must match training feature columns exactly (shape validation)
mappings.validation: Output shape: (forecast_horizon, 8) matching training; all features numeric; no NaN

mappings.label: Automated Anomaly Detection Schedule
mappings.formula: PM2 cron job runs every 15 minutes. Executes: (1) Validate outdoor sensor with fallback, (2) Run Isolation Forest anomaly detector, (3) Assess outdoor influence for each anomaly, (4) Log to logs/ml-anomalies.jsonl (JSON Lines format), (5) Filter alerts (critical_only if env var set), (6) Send webhooks (Slack/Discord if enabled), (7) POST to /api/alerts endpoint. Timeout: 60 seconds. Auto-restart: false (cron-only)
mappings.validation: Cron pattern: */15 * * * * (every 15 min); logs rotated daily; max execution time: 60s

mappings.label: Anomaly Logging Format
mappings.formula: JSON Lines format (one JSON object per line). Each entry: {timestamp: ISO8601, count: int, critical_count: int, warning_count: int, info_count: int, anomalies: [{zone, timestamp, indoor_temp, indoor_rh, indoor_vpd, outdoor_temp, outdoor_rh, temp_delta, outdoor_influence, anomaly_likelihood, expected_indoor, hvac_load, severity, reason}]}. Appended to file (no size limit, manual rotation recommended)
mappings.validation: File: logs/ml-anomalies.jsonl; encoding: UTF-8; format: JSON Lines (.jsonl); typical size: ~100KB/day

mappings.label: Webhook Alert Integration
mappings.formula: Sends Slack/Discord webhooks for critical/warning anomalies. Payload: {text: "🔴 ML Anomaly Detected", attachments: [{color: "danger"|"warning", fields: [{title, value}]}]}. Fields: Zone, Severity, Indoor Conditions (temp, RH, VPD), Outdoor Conditions (temp, RH), Reason (equipment failure description). Enabled via env var: ENABLE_WEBHOOKS=true, ALERT_WEBHOOK_URL=https://...
mappings.validation: Webhook URL must be https://; color: danger=critical, warning=warning; max retries: 3; timeout: 10s

mappings.label: GET /api/ml/anomalies
mappings.formula: Executes Python anomaly detector script with --json flag. Returns: {ok: true, count, critical_count, warning_count, info_count, anomalies: [], timestamp}. Anomaly objects include: zone, indoor_temp, outdoor_temp, temp_delta, outdoor_influence, anomaly_likelihood, severity, reason. Timeout: 30 seconds. Error handling: 503 if sklearn not installed, 500 if script fails
mappings.validation: Response status: 200 OK, 503 Service Unavailable, 500 Internal Error; timeout: 30s; requires Python3 + scikit-learn

mappings.label: GET /api/ml/forecast
mappings.formula: Query params: zone (required, URL-encoded), hours (1-4, default 2), metric (indoor_temp|indoor_rh, default indoor_temp). Executes Python forecast via subprocess. Returns: {ok: true, zone, metric, forecast_horizon_hours, current_value, current_timestamp, predictions: [{timestamp, value, lower_ci, upper_ci}], outdoor_conditions: {current, forecast}, model_info: {aic, training_samples}, timestamp}. Predictions array length: hours×12 (5-min intervals). Timeout: 60 seconds
mappings.validation: zone: must exist in env.json; hours: 1-4; metric: indoor_temp|indoor_rh; status: 200/400/500/504; timeout: 60s

mappings.label: Model AIC Score
mappings.formula: Akaike Information Criterion: AIC = 2k - 2ln(L), where k=number of parameters, L=likelihood. Lower is better. Balances model fit vs complexity. Typical range: -50 to -150 for good models. Used to compare different SARIMAX configurations (e.g., order=(2,0,1) vs (3,0,2)). AIC < -100 indicates excellent fit
mappings.validation: Range: -200 to 100; lower is better; typical good model: -50 to -150; excellent: < -100

mappings.label: Forecast Confidence Intervals
mappings.formula: 95% confidence intervals (2.5th and 97.5th percentiles). For each prediction: {lower_ci, upper_ci}. Interpretation: 95% probability true value falls within [lower_ci, upper_ci]. Wider intervals = higher uncertainty. Narrow intervals = high confidence. Width typically increases with forecast horizon (longer forecasts = more uncertainty). Example: prediction=22.4°C, lower_ci=21.8°C, upper_ci=23.0°C → 95% chance actual temp in 21.8-23.0°C range
mappings.validation: alpha: 0.01-0.10 (0.05 = 95% CI); lower_ci < value < upper_ci; width increases with horizon

mappings.label: Proactive HVAC Control
mappings.source: Forecast predictions used to pre-adjust HVAC before conditions deteriorate
mappings.formula: Query: GET /api/ml/forecast?zone=Grow%20Room%201&hours=2. Extract predictions[]. Find max predicted temp. If max > setpoint_max: trigger cooling increase NOW (before temp rises). If min < setpoint_min: trigger heating increase NOW. Prevents reactive control (waiting for problem to occur). Energy savings: gradual adjustment vs emergency response. Plant health: maintains optimal conditions proactively
mappings.validation: Forecast horizon: 1-4 hours optimal for HVAC response time; trigger threshold: setpoint ± tolerance (e.g., 24°C ± 1°C)

mappings.label: Equipment Failure Prediction
mappings.source: Anomaly detection + forecast divergence → equipment health monitoring
mappings.formula: Monitor: (1) Current anomaly_likelihood > 0.7 (critical equipment failure), (2) Forecast predictions diverge from expected (e.g., predicted 28°C when outdoor 15°C and HVAC should maintain 22°C), (3) Trend analysis: consecutive rising predictions despite HVAC active. Alert on-call engineer before complete failure. Schedule maintenance during low-risk periods. Example: Compressor degradation detected 2 hours before total failure
mappings.validation: Anomaly threshold: likelihood > 0.7; forecast divergence: predicted - expected > 5°C; trend: 3+ consecutive rising predictions
